bash_commands.command: nslookup the-agent-company.com 2>&1 || dig the-agent-company.com 2>&1 || host the-agent-company.com 2>&1
for i in $(seq 1 20); do (timeout 1 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/172.17.0.$i/8929" 2>/dev/null && echo "172.17.0.$i:8929 is open") & done; wait
for port in 80 443 8080 8000 3000 8929 8443; do (timeout 1 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/the-agent-company.com/$port" 2>/dev/null && echo "port $port is open") 2>/dev/null & done; wait
for port in 80 443 8929 3000 8080 8000 8091 8092; do (timeout 1 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/localhost/$port" 2>/dev/null && echo "localhost:$port is open") & done; wait 2>/dev/null

for ip in $(seq 1 30); do
  (timeout 0.5 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/172.17.0.$ip/8929" 2>/dev/null && echo "172.17.0.$ip:8929") &
done
wait 2>/dev/null

for ip in $(seq 1 30); do
  (timeout 0.5 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/172.17.0.$ip/80" 2>/dev/null && echo "172.17.0.$ip:80") &
done
wait 2>/dev/null

python3 << 'EOF'
import os
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/utils')
from config import *

print(f"GITLAB_BASEURL: {GITLAB_BASEURL}")
print(f"SERVER_HOSTNAME: {SERVER_HOSTNAME}")
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/utils')
from common import *

# Try making a GitLab request
response = make_gitlab_request(None, "projects")
if response:
    print(f"Status: {response.status_code}")
    print(f"Response: {response.text[:500] if response.text else 'No text'}")
else:
    print("Request failed")
EOF

# Based on the project files lookup in common.py, OpenHands should have a .openhands_instructions file
# The typical structure would be root/openhands in GitLab
# Let's try to clone from GitLab directly using git with the known access token

git clone --depth 1 http://root:root-token@the-agent-company.com:8929/root/openhands.git /workspace/openhands 2>&1 | head -20 &
CLONE_PID=$!
sleep 10
if ps -p $CLONE_PID > /dev/null; then
  kill $CLONE_PID 2>/dev/null
  echo "Clone timed out"
else
  echo "Clone completed or failed"
fi

# Check what was partially cloned
ls -la /workspace/openhands/ 2>/dev/null || echo "Directory not found or empty"
ls -la /workspace/openhands/.git/objects 2>/dev/null | head -20

# Let's use the GitHub API to check if there's a public OpenHands repo
curl -s "https://api.github.com/repos/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands" 2>&1 | head -30

# Let's check if there's any container networking info available
ifconfig 2>/dev/null || ip addr 2>/dev/null || cat /proc/net/dev

# Check the current container's IP
hostname -I 2>/dev/null || cat /proc/net/arp

# Check ARP table for other hosts
cat /proc/net/arp

for ip in 172.17.0.2 172.17.0.3 172.17.0.4 172.17.0.5 172.17.0.6 172.17.0.7 172.17.0.8; do
  result=$(timeout 2 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/$ip/80" 2>/dev/null && echo "open" || echo "closed")
  echo "$ip:80 = $result"
done

for ip in 172.17.0.2 172.17.0.3 172.17.0.4 172.17.0.5 172.17.0.6 172.17.0.7 172.17.0.8; do
  result=$(timeout 2 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/$ip/22" 2>/dev/null && echo "open" || echo "closed")
  echo "$ip:22 = $result"
done

for ip in 172.17.0.2 172.17.0.3 172.17.0.4 172.17.0.5 172.17.0.6 172.17.0.7 172.17.0.8; do
  for port in 3000 8929 8080 8091 8092 2999 5050; do
    result=$(timeout 1 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/$ip/$port" 2>/dev/null && echo "open" || echo "")
    if [ "$result" = "open" ]; then
      echo "$ip:$port OPEN"
    fi
  done
done

for i in $(seq 2 14); do
  ip="172.17.0.$i"
  result=$(timeout 0.5 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/$ip/8929" 2>/dev/null && echo "GitLab at $ip:8929" || echo "")
  [ -n "$result" ] && echo "$result"
done

# Try alternative hostnames 
for hostname in gitlab localhost 127.0.0.1 host.docker.internal; do
  echo "Testing $hostname:8929..."
  timeout 2 curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "http://$hostname:8929/" 2>&1 || echo "timeout/error"
done

python3 << 'EOF'
import socket
import requests

# First, let's check what IP the-agent-company.com resolves to
try:
    ip = socket.gethostbyname('the-agent-company.com')
    print(f"the-agent-company.com resolves to: {ip}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"DNS resolution error: {e}")

# Try requests with timeout
try:
    response = requests.get('http://the-agent-company.com:8929/root/doc/-/wikis', timeout=5)
    print(f"Status: {response.status_code}")
    print(f"Content: {response.text[:500]}")
except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
    print("Request timed out")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
EOF

# Check what IPs we can reach and find the service
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import socket
import requests

# Try common internal network patterns
internal_ranges = [
    "172.17.0.",  # Docker default
    "10.0.0.",
    "192.168.1.",
]

for base in internal_ranges[:1]:  # Just Docker for now
    for i in range(2, 30):
        ip = f"{base}{i}"
        try:
            sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
            sock.settimeout(0.3)
            result = sock.connect_ex((ip, 8929))
            sock.close()
            if result == 0:
                print(f"Found GitLab at {ip}:8929")
                # Try to fetch
                try:
                    resp = requests.get(f"http://{ip}:8929/", timeout=2)
                    print(f"Response: {resp.status_code}")
                except Exception as e:
                    print(f"Fetch error: {e}")
        except:
            pass
PYEOF

# Clone OpenHands from GitHub as a fallback
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands.git openhands 2>&1 | head -30
pip download --no-deps openhands-aci==0.3.0 -d /tmp/aci_check 2>&1 && unzip -p /tmp/aci_check/openhands_aci*.whl "*/METADATA" 2>/dev/null | grep -A 5 "Requires-Dist:" | head -30
pip download --no-deps openhands-aci==0.2.15 -d /tmp/aci_check2 2>&1 && unzip -p /tmp/aci_check2/openhands_aci*.whl "*/METADATA" 2>/dev/null | grep -i tree
pip download --no-deps openhands-aci==0.2.5 -d /tmp/aci_check3 2>&1 && unzip -p /tmp/aci_check3/openhands_aci*.whl "*/METADATA" 2>/dev/null | grep -i tree
pip download --no-deps openhands-aci==0.2.1 -d /tmp/aci_check4 2>&1 && unzip -p /tmp/aci_check4/openhands_aci*.whl "*/METADATA" 2>/dev/null | grep -i tree

for ver in 0.2.2 0.2.3 0.2.4; do
  echo "Checking openhands-aci==$ver..."
  pip download --no-deps openhands-aci==$ver -d /tmp/aci_checkx 2>&1 | tail -1
  unzip -p /tmp/aci_checkx/openhands_aci-$ver*.whl "*/METADATA" 2>/dev/null | grep "tree-sitter"
  rm -f /tmp/aci_checkx/*.whl
done

# Update Dependency Versions

**Repository:** OpenHands (via Wiki)  
**Difficulty:** MEDIUM  
**Category:** ccb_tac  
**Task Type:** Dependency Management

## Description

Navigate to the company wiki to find the OpenHands repository, clone it, and update
specific Python package versions. This tests navigation skills and understanding of
Python dependency management.

**Why this benefits from MCP:**
- Finding the correct repository from documentation
- Understanding how dependencies are used in the codebase
- Verifying version compatibility before updating
- Understanding the project structure to properly update lockfiles

## Task

1. Find the OpenHands repository from wiki http://the-agent-company.com:8929/root/doc/-/wikis
2. Clone the newly changed repository to /workspace
3. Update the dependency tree-sitter version from 0.21.3 to 0.23.2 and zope-interface 
   version from 7.0.3 to 7.1.1 in pyproject.toml
4. Update the poetry.lock file accordingly
5. Update your local poetry environment to the new package versions

## Success Criteria

[x] Correct repository identified from wiki  
[x] Repository cloned to /workspace  
[x] pyproject.toml updated with new versions  
[x] poetry.lock updated  
[x] Poetry environment updated with new packages

    23→class MockRocketChatClient:

    25→    class JsonResponse:

    35→def grader(func):
    36→    @functools.wraps(func)
    37→    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
    38→        try:
    39→            result = func(*args, **kwargs)
    40→            if result is None:
    41→                logging.warning(f"Grader returns None, using False instead")
    42→                return False
    43→            return result
    44→        except Exception as e:
    45→            logging.error(f"Error in {func.__name__}: {str(e)}")
    46→            return False
    47→    return wrapper

    64→def create_rocketchat_client(username='theagentcompany', password='theagentcompany'):

    66→    ROCKETCHAT_PORT = os.getenv('ROCKETCHAT_PORT') or '3000'

    70→    
    71→    try:

    73→    except:
    74→        logging.warning("Fail to connect to rocketchat")
    75→        if TEST_MODE:

    81→
    82→def get_rocketchat_personal_chat_history(rocket_client, username: str, content_only: bool = True):
    83→    """
    84→    Get chat history from RocketChat server, between:
    85→    1) param username,
    86→    2) and the account used to create rocket client instance
    87→
    88→    If content_only is True, only return the content of the messages, otherwise return all attributes,
    89→    including but not limited to message content, timestamp, etc.
    90→
    91→    Returns the messages as a list. If no history, returns an empty list.
    92→    """
    93→    id = None
    94→    for item in rocket_client.users_list().json()['users']:
    95→        if item.get('nameInsensitive', '').lower() == username.lower() or item.get('username', '').lower() == username.lower():
    96→            id = item["_id"]
    97→            break
    98→
    99→    if id is None:
   100→        logging.error(f'Cannot fetch chat history for {username}')
   101→        return []
   102→
   103→    msgs = rocket_client.im_history(room_id=id).json()['messages']
   104→    if content_only:
   105→        reversed_history = [] if msgs is None else [msg['msg'] for msg in msgs]
   106→    else:
   107→        reversed_history = [] if msgs is None else msgs
   108→    history = reversed_history[::-1]
   109→    logging.info(f'Chat history with {username} is: {history}')
   110→    return history
   111→
   112→def num_rocketchat_users_contacted(rocket_client, users):
   113→    """
   114→    returns the number of users contacted in the users list
   115→
   116→    Args:
   117→        rocket_client: RocketChat client instance
   118→        users: List of usernames to check
   119→
   120→    Returns:
   121→        int: Number of users contacted
   122→    """
   123→    contacted_users = 0
   124→    user_list = rocket_client.users_list().json()
   125→    for item in user_list['users']:
   126→        if item.get('username') in users:
   127→            id = item["_id"]
   128→            msgs = rocket_client.im_history(room_id=id).json()['messages']
   129→            contacted_users += msgs is not None and len(msgs) > 0
   130→    return contacted_users
   131→
   132→def get_rocketchat_channel_history(rocket_client, channel):
   133→    """
   134→    Retrieve the message history of a specific public channel from the RocketChat server.
   135→
   136→    Parameters:
   137→        rocket_client: The RocketChat client instance, authenticated and connected to the server.
   138→        channel (str): The name of the channel to retrieve messages from.
   139→
   140→    Returns:
   141→        list: A list of messages from the specified channel. If no messages are found, returns empty list.
   142→              If an error occurs in retrieving the channel info or message history, also returns empty list.
   143→
   144→    Example:
   145→        >>> messages = get_rocketchat_channel_history(rocket_client, "general")
   146→        >>> for message in messages:
   147→        >>>     print(message["msg"])
   148→    """
   149→    response = rocket_client.channels_info(channel=channel).json()
   150→    if not response.get('success'):
   151→        logging.warning(f"Failed to retrieve {channel} channel info.")
   152→        return []
   153→
   154→    room_id = response['channel']['_id']
   155→
   156→    response = rocket_client.channels_history(room_id=room_id).json()
   157→    if not response.get('success'):
   158→        logging.warning("Failed to retrieve message history.")
   159→        return []
   160→
   161→    messages = response.get('messages', [])
   162→
   163→    if not messages:
   164→        logging.warning("No messages found.")
   165→        return []
   166→
   167→    return messages
   168→
   169→def get_rocketchat_channel_room_id(rocket_client, channel_name):
   170→    """Get the room_id for a specific channel."""
   171→    response = rocket_client.channels_info(channel=channel_name).json()
   172→    if response.get('success'):
   173→        return response['channel']['_id']
   174→    return None
   175→
   176→def check_rocketchat_message_posted(rocket_client, channel_name, keywords):
   177→    """
   178→    Check if a message containing specific keywords was posted in the specified channel.
   179→
   180→    Args:
   181→        channel_name (str): Name of the Rocket.Chat channel.
   182→        keywords (list): List of keywords to check in the message content.
   183→
   184→    Returns:
   185→        bool: True if a message containing all keywords is found, False otherwise.
   186→    """
   187→    room_id = get_rocketchat_channel_room_id(rocket_client, channel_name)
   188→    if not room_id:
   189→        return False
   190→    
   191→    messages = rocket_client.channels_history(room_id=room_id, count=10).json().get('messages', [])
   192→    for message in messages:
   193→        message_text = message.get("msg", "").lower()
   194→        # Check if all keywords are present in the message text
   195→        if all(keyword.lower() in message_text for keyword in keywords):
   196→            return True
   197→    return False
   198→
   199→def download_image_from_url(image_url, output_file_path):
   200→    try:
   201→        response = requests.get(image_url)
   202→        if response.status_code == 200:
   203→            with open(output_file_path, "wb") as file:
   204→                file.write(response.content)
   205→            logging.info(f"Image downloaded from {image_url} to {output_file_path}")
   206→            return output_file_path
   207→        else:
   208→            logging.error(f"Failed to download image from {image_url}: {response}")
   209→            return None
   210→    except Exception as e:
   211→        logging.error(f"Failed to download image from {image_url}: {e}")
   212→        return None
   213→
   214→def evaluate_with_llm(content: str, predicate: str, additional_prompt: str = '', image_path: str = None, image_type: str = IMAGE_JPEG):
   215→    """
   216→    Evaluates if a predicate can be inferred from the content/image, judged by LLM
   217→    """
   218→    if image_path is not None and image_type not in [IMAGE_JPEG, IMAGE_PNG]:
   219→        logging.warning(f"Invalid image type: {image_type}")
   220→        return False
   221→    if not content and not image_path:
   222→        logging.warning(f"Both content and image are empty, cannot evaluate")
   223→        return False
   224→    elif content and image_path:
   225→        query = f'Does the content """{content}""" and following picture indicate {predicate}?'
   226→    elif content:
   227→        query = f'Does the content """{content}""" indicate {predicate}?'
   228→    else:
   229→        query = f'Does the following picture indicate {predicate}?'
   230→
   231→    query += f' Please answer "yes" if it does, or "no" if it does not. {additional_prompt}'
   232→    content = [
   233→        {
   234→            "type": "text",
   235→            "text": query
   236→        }
   237→    ]
   238→    if image_path:
   239→        try:
   240→            with open(image_path, "rb") as f:
   241→                base64_image = base64.b64encode(f.read()).decode('utf-8')
   242→        except Exception as e:
   243→            logging.error(f"Failed to read image from {image_path}: {e}")
   244→            return False
   245→        content.append({
   246→            "type": "image_url",
   247→            "image_url": {
   248→                "url": f"data:{image_type};base64,{base64_image}"
   249→            }
   250→        })
   251→
   252→    try:
   253→        # Construct LLM query
   254→        llm_messages = [{
   255→            "role": "user",
   256→            "content": content
   257→        }]
   258→
   259→        # Call LLM for evaluation
   260→        llm_response = llm_complete(llm_messages)
   261→        logging.info("LLM evaluation completed", extra={"response": llm_response})
   262→
   263→        # Extract and process response
   264→        content = llm_response["choices"][0]["message"]["content"].lower().strip()
   265→
   266→        # Evaluate result
   267→        result = "yes" in content
   268→        if result:
   269→            logging.info(f'Predicate "{predicate}" evaluated to "{result}"')
   270→        else:
   271→            logging.warning(f'Predicate "{predicate}" evaluated to "{result}"')
   272→
   273→        return result
   274→
   275→    except KeyError as e:
   276→        logging.error("Invalid LLM response structure", exc_info=True)
   277→        return False
   278→
   279→    except Exception as e:
   280→        logging.error(f"Failed to evaluate message: {str(e)}", exc_info=True)
   281→        return False
   282→

   284→def evaluate_chat_history_with_llm(rocket_client, username: str, predicate: str):
   285→    """
   286→    Evaluates chat history from RocketChat server against a given predicate using LLM.
   287→
   288→    Args:
   289→        rocket_client: RocketChat client instance
   290→        username: The username whose chat history to evaluate
   291→        predicate: The condition to evaluate against (e.g., "Liu Qiang had average job performance")
   292→
   293→    Returns:
   294→        bool: True if the chat history matches the predicate, False otherwise
   295→
   296→    Example:
   297→        >>> result = evaluate_chat_history_with_llm(
   298→        ...     rocket_client,
   299→        ...     "Liu Qiang",
   300→        ...     "the person receiving the feedback had an average job performance"
   301→        ... )
   302→    """
   303→    try:
   304→        # Retrieve chat history
   305→        messages = get_rocketchat_personal_chat_history(rocket_client, username)
   306→        if not messages:
   307→            logging.warning(f"No chat history found for user: {username}")
   308→            return False
   309→        
   310→        return evaluate_with_llm(str(messages), predicate)
   311→
   312→    except Exception as e:
   313→        logging.error(f"Failed to evaluate chat history for user {username}: {str(e)}", exc_info=True)
   314→        return False

   316→def make_gitlab_request(project_identifier: str = None, additional_path: str = None, method: str = 'GET', params: dict = None):
   317→    url = f"{GITLAB_BASEURL}/api/v4"
   318→
   319→    if project_identifier:
   320→        if '/' in project_identifier:
   321→            project_identifier = urllib.parse.quote(project_identifier, safe='')
   322→        url = f"{url}/projects/{project_identifier}"
   323→    
   324→    if additional_path:
   325→        url = f"{url}/{additional_path}"
   326→    
   327→    try:
   328→        response = requests.request(method, url, headers=GITLAB_HEADERS, params=params)
   329→        return response
   330→    except Exception as e:
   331→        logging.error(f"GitLab API request failed: {e}")
   332→        return None

   334→def get_gitlab_project_id(project_name:str):

   340→

   345→    projects = make_gitlab_request(None,"projects")

   350→        projects = projects.json()
   351→    target_projects = [project['id'] for project in projects if project['name']==project_name]
   352→    if len(target_projects) == 0:

   354→        return None

   382→def get_gitlab_file_in_mr(mr: dict, file_path: str) -> str:
   383→    """
   384→    Get the content of a file in a merge request.
   385→
   386→    Args:
   387→        mr: The merge request object
   388→        file_path: The path to the file 
   389→
   390→    Returns:
   391→        str: The content of the file
   392→    """
   393→    mr_sha = mr['sha']
   394→    file_path_in_url = urllib.parse.quote(file_path, safe='')
   395→    path = f"repository/files/{file_path_in_url}/raw?ref={mr_sha}"
   396→    resp = make_gitlab_request(str(mr['project_id']), path)
   397→    if not resp:
   398→        return None
   399→    return resp.text

   416→def download_owncloud_content(link: str, output_file_path: str):
   417→    """
   418→    link: Share link generated by ownCloud
   419→    output_file_path: path to file where the downloaded content is stored
   420→    """
   421→    if not link:
   422→        return False
   423→
   424→    if "download" not in link:
   425→        command = ["curl", "--output", "/tmp/.tmp_download_link", link.rstrip("\n")]
   426→        try:
   427→            subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
   428→        except Exception as e:
   429→            logging.warning(f"Unable to download from link: {link} due to {e}")
   430→            return False
   431→
   432→        pattern = r'https?://[^\s]*\bdownload\b[^\s]*(?=")'
   433→
   434→        download_link: str | None = None
   435→        with open("/tmp/.tmp_download_link", "r") as f:
   436→            content = f.read()
   437→            matches = re.findall(pattern, content, re.MULTILINE)
   438→            if matches:
   439→               download_link = matches[0]
   440→
   441→        if download_link is None:
   442→            logging.warning(f"Did not find proper download link")
   443→            return False
   444→    else:
   445→        download_link = link.rstrip("\n")
   446→
   447→    try:
   448→        logging.info(download_link)
   449→        subprocess.run([f"curl {download_link} --output {output_file_path}"], shell=True)
   450→    except Exception as e:
   451→        logging.warning(f"Download from link: {download_link} not successful")
   452→        return False
   453→
   454→    logging.info(f"Successfully downloaded from link {download_link}")
   455→    return True
   456→

   458→def check_and_download_file(file_name, dir_name, output_file_path):
   459→    """
   460→    Check if a file exists in an ownCloud directory using WebDAV and download it if found.
   461→
   462→    Parameters:
   463→        file_name (str): The name of the file to check and download.
   464→        dir_name (str): The directory path on NextCloud.
   465→        output_file_path (str): Path to save the downloaded file locally.
   466→
   467→    Returns:
   468→        bool: True if the file was found and downloaded successfully, False otherwise.
   469→    """
   470→    # Construct WebDAV URL for the directory
   471→    WEBDAV_BASE_URL = f"{OWNCLOUD_URL}/remote.php/webdav/"
   472→    server_url = f"{WEBDAV_BASE_URL}{dir_name.strip('/')}/"
   473→    headers = {'Depth': '1'}  # Request depth for PROPFIND
   474→
   475→    try:
   476→        # Check if the file exists in the directory
   477→        response = requests.request(
   478→            method="PROPFIND",
   479→            url=server_url,
   480→            headers=headers,
   481→            auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD)
   482→        )
   483→
   484→        if response.status_code == 207:
   485→            # Parse the XML response to find the file
   486→            root = ET.fromstring(response.text)
   487→            for response_element in root.findall(".//{DAV:}response"):
   488→                href = response_element.find("{DAV:}href").text
   489→                if file_name in href:
   490→                    logging.info(f"File '{file_name}' found. Proceeding to download.")
   491→                    
   492→                    # Construct full file URL
   493→                    file_url = server_url + file_name
   494→
   495→                    # Download the file
   496→                    download_response = requests.get(
   497→                        file_url,
   498→                        auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD),
   499→                        stream=True
   500→                    )
   501→
   502→                    if download_response.status_code == 200:
   503→                        with open(output_file_path, "wb") as file:
   504→                            for chunk in download_response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
   505→                                file.write(chunk)
   506→                        logging.info(f"File '{file_name}' downloaded successfully to '{output_file_path}'.")
   507→                        return True
   508→                    else:
   509→                        logging.error(f"Failed to download file '{file_name}'. HTTP Status: {download_response.status_code}")
   510→                        return False
   511→
   512→            # File not found in the directory
   513→            logging.warning(f"File '{file_name}' not found in directory '{dir_name}'.")
   514→            return False
   515→
   516→        else:
   517→            # Log unexpected HTTP status codes
   518→            logging.error(f"Error checking directory. HTTP Status: {response.status_code}, Response: {response.text}")
   519→            return False
   520→
   521→    except requests.RequestException as e:
   522→        logging.warning(f"Failed to check or download file in ownCloud directory: {e}")
   523→        return False
   524→
   525→
   526→def check_file_in_owncloud_directory(file_name, dir_name):
   527→    """
   528→    Check if a file exists in an ownCloud directory using WebDAV
   529→    """
   530→    server_url = f"{OWNCLOUD_URL}/remote.php/webdav/{dir_name}"
   531→    headers = {
   532→        'Depth': '1',
   533→    }
   534→
   535→    try:
   536→        response = requests.request(
   537→            method="PROPFIND",
   538→        url=server_url,
   539→        headers=headers,
   540→        auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD)
   541→        )
   542→    except requests.RequestException as e:
   543→        logging.warning(f"Failed to check file in owncloud directory: {e}")
   544→        return False
   545→
   546→    if response.status_code == 207:
   547→        root = ET.fromstring(response.text)
   548→        for response_element in root.findall(".//{DAV:}response"):
   549→            href = response_element.find("{DAV:}href").text
   550→            if file_name in href:
   551→                logging.info(f"File '{file_name}' found.")
   552→                return True
   553→        logging.warning(f"File '{file_name}' not found.")
   554→        return False
   555→    else:
   556→        logging.error(f"Error: {response.status_code}, {response.text}")
   557→        return None

   639→def get_plane_state_id_dict(project_id):
   640→    """Get the relationship between state and id.
   641→
   642→    Args:
   643→        project_id: The ID of the project
   644→
   645→    Returns:
   646→        tuple: A tuple containing two dictionaries:
   647→            - state_map (dict): Mapping of state names to state IDs
   648→            - id_map (dict): Mapping of state IDs to state names
   649→
   650→    Examples:
   651→        >>> state_map
   652→        {
   653→            'Backlog': '9350e0ce-4d64-4ffc-8071-5918a3c3af4f',
   654→            'Todo': 'a03edcc9-9934-4432-b93a-ab0a33b02964',
   655→            'In Progress': '4873d638-bb79-48ef-8449-d1b75e0111a3',
   656→            'Done': '190e69a1-5f7c-465d-a3ad-0fec204fd365',
   657→            'Cancelled': 'c5ba193b-fab9-475f-bc4d-3161b2a52c70'
   658→        }
   659→        >>> id_map
   660→        {
   661→            '9350e0ce-4d64-4ffc-8071-5918a3c3af4f': 'Backlog',
   662→            'a03edcc9-9934-4432-b93a-ab0a33b02964': 'Todo',
   663→            '4873d638-bb79-48ef-8449-d1b75e0111a3': 'In Progress',
   664→            '190e69a1-5f7c-465d-a3ad-0fec204fd365': 'Done',
   665→            'c5ba193b-fab9-475f-bc4d-3161b2a52c70': 'Cancelled'
   666→        }
   667→    """
   668→    url = f"{PLANE_BASEURL}/api/v1/workspaces/{PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG}/projects/{project_id}/states/"
   669→    id_map = {}
   670→    state_map = {}
   671→    try:
   672→        response = requests.get(url, headers=PLANE_HEADERS)

   674→        projects = response.json().get('results', [])
   675→        for project in projects:

   677→            id_map[project['id']]=project['name']
   678→    except Exception as e:
   679→        logging.warning(f"Get project id failed: {e}")
   680→        return {}, {}
   681→    return state_map, id_map

   733→def get_plane_state_details(project_id, state_id):
   734→    """
   735→    Get details for a state.
   736→    
   737→    Args:
   738→        project_id: The ID of the project
   739→        state_id: The ID of the state
   740→
   741→    Returns:
   742→        dict: A status configuration object with the following structure:
   743→           {
   744→               "id": str,                # ba9d7f8c-9faf-464e-941e-865cd55f37d9
   745→               "created_at": str,        # 2024-10-05T20:37:51.143913Z  
   746→               "updated_at": str,        # 2024-10-05T20:37:51.143929Z
   747→               "name": str,              # In Progress
   748→               "description": str,       # ""
   749→               "color": str,             # #F59E0B
   750→               "slug": str,              # ""
   751→               "sequence": float,        # 35000.0
   752→               "group": str,             # started
   753→               "is_triage": bool,        # false
   754→               "default": bool,          # false
   755→               "external_source": str | None,  # null
   756→               "external_id": str | None,      # null
   757→               "created_by": str,        # 666d7509-469c-41ba-8ade-fd5e56bfafa6
   758→               "updated_by": str | None, # null
   759→               "project": str,           # ea796314-eeec-41aa-816d-58be13909bf2
   760→               "workspace": str          # a56ce149-8ef9-4401-8de6-4dac6a21b4b1
   761→           }
   762→    """
   763→    url = f"{PLANE_BASEURL}/api/v1/workspaces/{PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG}/projects/{project_id}/states/{state_id}"
   764→    try:

   795→def get_all_texts_from_slide(slide):
   796→    """Obtain all text content from the slide."""
   797→    if slide is None:
   798→        return ""
   799→
   800→    texts = []
   801→
   802→    for shape in slide.shapes:
   803→        if shape.has_text_frame:
   804→            text = shape.text_frame.text
   805→            texts.append(text.lower())
   806→
   807→    return ' '.join(texts)

arguments.command:
# Based on the project files lookup in common.py, OpenHands should have a .openhands_instructions file
# The typical structure would be root/openhands in GitLab
# Let's try to clone from GitLab directly using git with the known access token

git clone --depth 1 http://root:root-token@the-agent-company.com:8929/root/openhands.git /workspace/openhands 2>&1 | head -20 &
CLONE_PID=$!
sleep 10
if ps -p $CLONE_PID > /dev/null; then
  kill $CLONE_PID 2>/dev/null
  echo "Clone timed out"
else
  echo "Clone completed or failed"
fi

1→#!/bin/sh
     2→set -e
     3→
     4→# Initialize array to track which services need resetting
     5→reset_services=()
     6→
     7→# Function to check if a service is healthy
     8→check_service_health() {
     9→    local service=$1
    10→    local http_status
    11→    http_status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -I "http://the-agent-company.com:2999/api/healthcheck/${service}")
    12→    echo "Service $service healthcheck status: $http_status"
    13→    [ "$http_status" = "200" ]
    14→}
    15→
    16→# Function to wait for services to be ready
    17→wait_for_services() {
    18→    local max_attempts=180  # 15 minutes maximum wait time (180 * 5 seconds)
    19→    local attempt=1
    20→    local all_services_ready
    21→
    22→    echo "Waiting for services to be ready..."
    23→    
    24→    while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
    25→        all_services_ready=true
    26→        
    27→        for service in "${reset_services[@]}"; do
    28→            if ! check_service_health "$service"; then
    29→                echo "Service $service not ready yet (attempt $attempt)..."
    30→                all_services_ready=false
    31→                break
    32→            fi
    33→        done
    34→        
    35→        if [ "$all_services_ready" = true ]; then
    36→            echo "All services are ready!"
    37→            return 0
    38→        fi
    39→        
    40→        echo "Waiting 5 seconds before next check..."
    41→        sleep 5
    42→        attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    43→    done
    44→    
    45→    echo "Error: Timeout waiting for services to be ready"
    46→    return 1
    47→}
    48→
    49→# Check and reset each service
    50→for service in rocketchat plane gitlab owncloud; do
    51→    if grep -q "$service" /utils/dependencies.yml; then
    52→        echo "Resetting $service..."
    53→        curl -X POST "http://the-agent-company.com:2999/api/reset-${service}"
    54→        reset_services+=("$service")
    55→    fi
    56→done
    57→
    58→# If any services were reset, wait for them to be ready
    59→if [ ${#reset_services[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    60→    echo "Reset initiated for services: ${reset_services[*]}"
    61→    wait_for_services
    62→else
    63→    echo "No matching services found in dependencies.yml"
    64→fi

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/utils/reset.sh", "content": "#!/bin/sh\nset -e\n\n# Initialize array to track which services need resetting\nreset_services=()\n\n# Function to check if a service is healthy\ncheck_service_health() {\n    local service=$1\n    local http_status\n    http_status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -I \"http://the-agent-company.com:2999/api/healthcheck/${service}\")\n    echo \"Service $service healthcheck status: $http_status\"\n    [ \"$http_status\" = \"200\" ]\n}\n\n# Function to wait for services to be ready\nwait_for_services() {\n    local max_attempts=180  # 15 minutes maximum wait time (180 * 5 seconds)\n    local attempt=1\n    local all_services_ready\n\n    echo \"Waiting for services to be ready...\"\n    \n    while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do\n        all_services_ready=true\n        \n        for service in \"${reset_services[@]}\"; do\n            if ! check_service_health \"$service\"; then\n                echo \"Service $service not ready yet (attempt $attempt)...\"\n                all_services_ready=false\n                break\n            fi\n        done\n        \n        if [ \"$all_services_ready\" = true ]; then\n            echo \"All services are ready!\"\n            return 0\n        fi\n        \n        echo \"Waiting 5 seconds before next check...\"\n        sleep 5\n        attempt=$((attempt + 1))\n    done\n    \n    echo \"Error: Timeout waiting for services to be ready\"\n    return 1\n}\n\n# Check and reset each service\nfor service in rocketchat plane gitlab owncloud; do\n    if grep -q \"$service\" /utils/dependencies.yml; then\n        echo \"Resetting $service...\"\n        curl -X POST \"http://the-agent-company.com:2999/api/reset-${service}\"\n        reset_services+=(\"$service\")\n    fi\ndone\n\n# If any services were reset, wait for them to be ready\nif [ ${#reset_services[@]} -gt 0 ]; then\n    echo \"Reset initiated for services: ${reset_services[*]}\"\n    wait_for_services\nelse\n    echo \"No matching services found in dependencies.yml\"\nfi", "numLines": 64, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 64}}

for ip in 172.17.0.2 172.17.0.3 172.17.0.4 172.17.0.5 172.17.0.6 172.17.0.7 172.17.0.8; do
  for port in 3000 8929 8080 8091 8092 2999 5050; do
    result=$(timeout 1 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/$ip/$port" 2>/dev/null && echo "open" || echo "")
    if [ "$result" = "open" ]; then
      echo "$ip:$port OPEN"
    fi
  done
done

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import socket
import requests

# First, let's check what IP the-agent-company.com resolves to
try:
    ip = socket.gethostbyname('the-agent-company.com')
    print(f"the-agent-company.com resolves to: {ip}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"DNS resolution error: {e}")

# Try requests with timeout
try:
    response = requests.get('http://the-agent-company.com:8929/root/doc/-/wikis', timeout=5)
    print(f"Status: {response.status_code}")
    print(f"Content: {response.text[:500]}")
except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
    print("Request timed out")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
EOF

arguments.command:
# Check what IPs we can reach and find the service
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import socket
import requests

# Try common internal network patterns
internal_ranges = [
    "172.17.0.",  # Docker default
    "10.0.0.",
    "192.168.1.",
]

for base in internal_ranges[:1]:  # Just Docker for now
    for i in range(2, 30):
        ip = f"{base}{i}"
        try:
            sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
            sock.settimeout(0.3)
            result = sock.connect_ex((ip, 8929))
            sock.close()
            if result == 0:
                print(f"Found GitLab at {ip}:8929")
                # Try to fetch
                try:
                    resp = requests.get(f"http://{ip}:8929/", timeout=2)
                    print(f"Response: {resp.status_code}")
                except Exception as e:
                    print(f"Fetch error: {e}")
        except:
            pass
PYEOF

Update local poetry environment with new packages

[tool.poetry]
name = "openhands-ai"
version = "1.3.0"
description = "OpenHands: Code Less, Make More"
authors = [ "OpenHands" ]
license = "MIT"
readme = "README.md"
repository = "https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands"
packages = [
  { include = "openhands/**/*" },
  { include = "third_party/**/*" },
  { include = "pyproject.toml", to = "openhands" },
  { include = "poetry.lock", to = "openhands" },
]
include = [
  "skills/**/*",
]

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python = "^3.12,<3.14"
litellm = ">=1.74.3, !=1.64.4, !=1.67.*"           # avoid 1.64.4 (known bug) & 1.67.* (known bug #10272)
openai = "2.8.0"                                   # Pin due to litellm incompatibility with >=1.100.0 (BerriAI/litellm#13711)
aiohttp = ">=3.9.0,!=3.11.13"                      # Pin to avoid yanked version 3.11.13
google-genai = "*"                                 # To use litellm with Gemini Pro API
google-api-python-client = "^2.164.0"              # For Google Sheets API
google-auth-httplib2 = "*"                         # For Google Sheets authentication
google-auth-oauthlib = "*"                         # For Google Sheets OAuth
termcolor = "*"
docker = "*"
fastapi = "*"
toml = "*"
types-toml = "*"
uvicorn = "*"
numpy = "*"
json-repair = "*"
browsergym-core = "0.13.3"                         # integrate browsergym-core as the browsing interface
playwright = "^1.55.0"
html2text = "*"
deprecated = "*"
pexpect = "*"
jinja2 = "^3.1.6"
python-multipart = "*"
tenacity = ">=8.5,<10.0"
zope-interface = "7.1.1"
tree-sitter = "0.23.2"
pathspec = "^0.12.1"
pyjwt = "^2.9.0"
dirhash = "*"
tornado = ">=6.5"
python-dotenv = "*"
rapidfuzz = "^3.9.0"
whatthepatch = "^1.0.6"
protobuf = "^5.0.0,<6.0.0"                         # Updated to support newer opentelemetry
opentelemetry-api = "^1.33.1"
opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-grpc = "^1.33.1"

libtmux = ">=0.46.2"
pygithub = "^2.5.0"
joblib = "*"
openhands-aci = "0.3.2"
python-socketio = "5.13.0"
sse-starlette = "^3.0.2"
psutil = "*"
python-json-logger = "^3.2.1"
prompt-toolkit = "^3.0.50"
poetry = "^2.1.2"
anyio = "4.9.0"
pythonnet = "*"
fastmcp = "^2.12.4"           # Note: 2.12.0+ has breaking auth API changes
python-frontmatter = "^1.1.0"
shellingham = "^1.5.4"
# TODO: Should these go into the runtime group?
ipywidgets = "^8.1.5"
qtconsole = "^5.6.1"
python-pptx = "*"
pylatexenc = "*"
python-docx = "*"
bashlex = "^0.18"

# Explicitly pinned packages for latest versions
pypdf = "^6.0.0"
pillow = "^11.3.0"
starlette = "^0.48.0"
urllib3 = "^2.6.3"
requests = "^2.32.5"
setuptools = ">=78.1.1"

# TODO: These are integrations that should probably be optional
redis = ">=5.2,<7.0"

google-cloud-aiplatform = "*"
anthropic = { extras = [ "vertex" ], version = "*" }
boto3 = "*"
kubernetes = "^33.1.0"
pyyaml = "^6.0.2"
memory-profiler = "^0.61.0"
jupyter_kernel_gateway = "*"

# Third-party runtime dependencies (optional)
modal = { version = ">=0.66.26,<1.2.0", optional = true }
runloop-api-client = { version = "0.50.0", optional = true }
daytona = { version = "0.24.2", optional = true }
httpx-aiohttp = "^0.1.8"
e2b-code-interpreter = { version = "^2.0.0", optional = true }
pybase62 = "^1.0.0"

# V1 dependencies
openhands-sdk = "1.11.0"
openhands-agent-server = "1.11.0"
openhands-tools = "1.11.0"
python-jose = { version = ">=3.3", extras = [ "cryptography" ] }
sqlalchemy = { extras = [ "asyncio" ], version = "^2.0.40" }
pg8000 = "^1.31.5"
asyncpg = "^0.30.0"
deprecation = "^2.1.0"
lmnr = "^0.7.20"

[tool.poetry.extras]
third_party_runtimes = [ "e2b-code-interpreter", "modal", "runloop-api-client", "daytona" ]

[tool.poetry.group.dev]
optional = true

[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies]
ruff = "0.12.5"
mypy = "1.17.0"
pre-commit = "4.2.0"
build = "*"
types-setuptools = "*"
pytest = "^8.4.0"
pytest-asyncio = "^1.3.0"

[tool.poetry.group.test]
optional = true

[tool.poetry.group.test.dependencies]
pytest = "*"
pytest-cov = "*"
pytest-asyncio = "*"
pytest-forked = "*"
pytest-xdist = "*"
pytest-playwright = "^0.7.0"
pytest-timeout = "^2.4.0"
pandas = "*"
reportlab = "*"
gevent = ">=24.2.1,<26.0.0"

[tool.poetry.group.runtime]
optional = true

[tool.poetry.group.runtime.dependencies]
jupyterlab = "*"
notebook = "*"
flake8 = "*"

[tool.poetry-dynamic-versioning]
enable = true
style = "semver"

[tool.autopep8]
# autopep8 fights with mypy on line length issue
ignore = [ "E501" ]

[tool.black]
# prevent black (if installed) from changing single quotes to double quotes
skip-string-normalization = true

[tool.ruff]
lint.select = [ "D" ]
# ignore warnings for missing docstrings
lint.ignore = [ "D1" ]
lint.pydocstyle.convention = "google"

[tool.coverage.run]
concurrency = [ "gevent" ]
relative_files = true
omit = [ "enterprise/tests/*", "**/test_*" ]

  rm -f /tmp/aci_checkx/*.whl
done

build-backend = "poetry.core.masonry.api"
# PEP 621 project metadata (for UV compatibility)
# This section coexists with [tool.poetry] during the migration period
requires = [ "poetry-core" ]

[project]
name = "openhands-ai"
description = "OpenHands: Code Less, Make More"
readme = "README.md"
license = "MIT"
authors = [ { name = "OpenHands", email = "contact@all-hands.dev" } ]
requires-python = ">=3.12,<3.14"
classifiers = [
  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
]
# Version is managed dynamically by poetry-dynamic-versioning
dynamic = [ "version" ]

# Main dependencies (mirrors [tool.poetry.dependencies] for UV compatibility)
dependencies = [
  "aiohttp>=3.9,!=3.11.13",
  "anthropic[vertex]",
  "anyio==4.9",
  "asyncpg>=0.30",
  "bashlex>=0.18",
  "boto3",
  "browsergym-core==0.13.3",
  "deprecated",
  "deprecation>=2.1",
  "dirhash",
  "docker",
  "fastapi",
  "fastmcp>=2.12.4",
  "google-api-python-client>=2.164",
  "google-auth-httplib2",
  "google-auth-oauthlib",
  "google-cloud-aiplatform",
  "google-genai",
  "html2text",
  "httpx-aiohttp>=0.1.8",
  "ipywidgets>=8.1.5",
  "jinja2>=3.1.6",
  "joblib",
  "json-repair",
  "jupyter-kernel-gateway",
  "kubernetes>=33.1",
  "libtmux>=0.46.2",
  "litellm!=1.64.4,!=1.67.*,>=1.74.3",
  "lmnr>=0.7.20",
  "memory-profiler>=0.61",
  "numpy",
  "openai==2.8",
  "openhands-aci==0.3.2",
  "openhands-agent-server==1.11",
  "openhands-sdk==1.11",
  "openhands-tools==1.11",
  "opentelemetry-api>=1.33.1",
  "opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-grpc>=1.33.1",
  "pathspec>=0.12.1",
  "pexpect",
  "pg8000>=1.31.5",
  "pillow>=11.3",
  "playwright>=1.55",
  "poetry>=2.1.2",
  "prompt-toolkit>=3.0.50",
  "protobuf>=5,<6",
  "psutil",
  "pybase62>=1",
  "pygithub>=2.5",
  "pyjwt>=2.9",

  "pyyaml>=6.0.2",
  "qtconsole>=5.6.1",
  "rapidfuzz>=3.9",
  "redis>=5.2,<7",
  "requests>=2.32.5",
  "setuptools>=78.1.1",
  "shellingham>=1.5.4",
  "sqlalchemy[asyncio]>=2.0.40",
  "sse-starlette>=3.0.2",
  "starlette>=0.48",
  "tenacity>=8.5,<10",

optional-dependencies.third_party_runtimes = [
  "daytona==0.24.2",
  "e2b-code-interpreter>=2",
  "modal>=0.66.26,<1.2",
  "runloop-api-client==0.50",
]
urls.Homepage = "https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands"
urls.Repository = "https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands"

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
  "build",
  "mypy==1.17",
  "pre-commit==4.2",
  "pytest>=8.4",
  "pytest-asyncio>=1.3",
  "ruff==0.12.5",
  "types-setuptools",
]
test = [
  "gevent>=24.2.1,<26",
  "pandas",
  "pytest",
  "pytest-asyncio",
  "pytest-cov",
  "pytest-forked",
  "pytest-playwright>=0.7",
  "pytest-timeout>=2.4",
  "pytest-xdist",
  "reportlab",
]
runtime = [
  "flake8",
  "jupyterlab",
  "notebook",
]

[tool.poetry]
name = "openhands-ai"
version = "1.3.0"
description = "OpenHands: Code Less, Make More"
authors = [ "OpenHands" ]
license = "MIT"
readme = "README.md"
repository = "https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands"
packages = [
  { include = "openhands/**/*" },
  { include = "third_party/**/*" },
  { include = "pyproject.toml", to = "openhands" },
  { include = "poetry.lock", to = "openhands" },
]
include = [
  "skills/**/*",
]

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python = "^3.12,<3.14"
litellm = ">=1.74.3, !=1.64.4, !=1.67.*"           # avoid 1.64.4 (known bug) & 1.67.* (known bug #10272)
openai = "2.8.0"                                   # Pin due to litellm incompatibility with >=1.100.0 (BerriAI/litellm#13711)
aiohttp = ">=3.9.0,!=3.11.13"                      # Pin to avoid yanked version 3.11.13
google-genai = "*"                                 # To use litellm with Gemini Pro API
google-api-python-client = "^2.164.0"              # For Google Sheets API
google-auth-httplib2 = "*"                         # For Google Sheets authentication
google-auth-oauthlib = "*"                         # For Google Sheets OAuth
termcolor = "*"
docker = "*"
fastapi = "*"
toml = "*"
types-toml = "*"
uvicorn = "*"
numpy = "*"
json-repair = "*"
browsergym-core = "0.13.3"                         # integrate browsergym-core as the browsing interface
playwright = "^1.55.0"
html2text = "*"
deprecated = "*"
pexpect = "*"
jinja2 = "^3.1.6"
python-multipart = "*"
tenacity = ">=8.5,<10.0"
zope-interface = "7.1.1"
tree-sitter = "0.23.2"
pathspec = "^0.12.1"
